title: Internal Representations: Alpha-numerical Codes, Number Bases, BCD, Fixed-point

definitions.description: American Standard Code for Information Interchange: 7-bit character encoding (plus one parity bit). 'a'=97, 'A'=65, '0'=48.
Universal character encoding supporting non-Latin scripts (extension of ASCII).
A number representation where each position has an implicit weight (power of the base); value = Σ a_i * d^i.
Each decimal digit is represented by 4 bits; used in business/financial applications.
Each n-bit string represents the value given by binary writing minus k; the representable interval is [-k, 2^n - k - 1].
The leftmost bit encodes the sign; remaining bits encode the magnitude. Has two representations for 0.
Negative numbers are obtained by inverting all bits of the positive representation. Has two representations for 0.
Negative numbers are obtained by inverting all bits and adding 1. Only one representation for 0; addition uses the same hardware as unsigned.
The smallest non-zero difference between consecutive representable numbers; equals 2^(-m) for an n+m bit fixed-point representation.

formulas.name: Base conversion formula
formulas.expression: N = Σ a_i * d^i  (i from -m to n-1)

formulas.name: BCD addition correction
formulas.description: If the sum of two BCD digits exceeds 9, add 6 (0110) to get the correct BCD result.

algorithms.name: Base-10 to base-d integer conversion
algorithms.description: Repeated division by d, collect remainders bottom-up.

algorithms.name: Base-10 to base-d fractional conversion
algorithms.description: Repeated multiplication by d, collect integer parts top-down (may produce a repeating sequence).

algorithms.name: Two's complement negation
algorithms.description: Invert all bits and add 1 (equivalently: the C2 representation of -q equals q + 2^N for an N-bit representation).

algorithms.name: C1 addition
algorithms.description: Add using a classical adder; then add the carry-out bit to the result (end-around carry).

algorithms.name: C2 addition
algorithms.description: Add using a classical adder; the carry-out is simply ignored (no correction needed).

examples.name: Excess-5 table on 4 bits
examples.description: Shows values from -5 to 10 with their 4-bit encodings

exercises: Prove that C1 and C2 representations have a negative value when the sign bit is 1
Show why adding 6 corrects BCD addition when the sum exceeds 9
Derive the C2 N-bit representation of a negative integer q as 2^N - |q|
learning_objectives: Explain why character encodings are needed and how ASCII works
Perform base conversions between binary, octal, decimal, hexadecimal
Understand BCD representation and correct BCD addition
Explain excess-k representation and its representable interval
Compare sign-magnitude, one's complement, and two's complement
Determine the representable interval and precision of a fixed-point encoding
Perform negation and addition in C1 and C2
Explain why C2 is the most widely used integer representation
